title: Project: My Invitation
objectives: Create a simple invitation card for a celebration
Practice writing a short message including greeting, date, place, and purpose
Develop creativity through drawing and decorating

key_concepts.explanation: An invitation card includes: a greeting to the person, the event details (what, when, where), and the sender's name. Example: 'Dear Sara, please come to my celebration!' signed 'From, Noura.'

solution_steps: 1. Fold a piece of paper to make a card
2. Draw and colour Eid decorations on your card
3. Write a short message to your friend
4. Example: 'Dear Sara, please come to my celebration!'
5. Add your name at the end
7. Decorate your card with stars or stickers
8. Show your card and read your message aloud
examples.answer: A completed invitation card with Eid decorations, a written message, and the student's name
examples.visual_description: An envelope with blue balloons around it, showing a card with handwritten instructions

teaching_notes: This project reinforces writing skills from the unit. Students create a real invitation card, practising the format: greeting + message + name. Encourage creativity in decorating. Have students present their cards to the class to practise speaking.
